blocks.content:
## Foundations

*The modern web platform stack*

Build the mental model for how modern web applications actually run. Master the browser rendering pipeline, frontend frameworks, build systems, container and sandbox technologies, and the networking stack from DNS to edge.

## Modules in This Level

### F.1 How Browsers Execute Code

Those two seconds contain one of the most complex pipelines in software. Understanding it is not optional for a Platform PM — every performance complaint, every "why is preview slow," every decision a

### F.2 Modern Frontend Frameworks

Raw HTML/CSS/JS works fine for a landing page. It breaks down the moment you have state — data that changes and a UI that must reflect that change. Without a framework, keeping the DOM synchronized wi

### F.3 Build Systems & Bundlers

The gap between "code an AI wrote" and "code a browser can run" is not small. Browsers speak ES5-compatible JavaScript and standard CSS. AI-generated code is written in TypeScript, JSX, modern ES2022+

### F.4 Containers, VMs, and Sandboxes

This is the defining infrastructure problem of the AI code-gen product category. The answer you choose determines your cost structure, your startup latency, your language support, your security postur

### F.5 DNS, CDNs, and Edge Networks

Deployment is the moment Make's output becomes real. Before deployment, it's a preview — isolated, ephemeral, forgiving. After deployment, it's a product with a URL, with users, with latency expectati
